uses: Podagrostis trichodes, commonly known as needle grass, has been used in traditional medicine due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's extracts have been studied for their ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi. In some cultures, Podagrostis trichodes has been used to treat various ailments, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using the plant for medicinal purposes. More studies are required to fully understand the potential benefits and risks of using Podagrostis trichodes as a treatment.
